summary_one_line: Input must declare a namespaced element with a non-NULL URI whose prefix is processed so element-namespace resolution is reached.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element carrying a namespace prefix bound to a non-NULL URI (so URI != NULL at the blocker) where the namespace-building loop runs (nb_namespaces>0) and the element actually reaches the namespace-resolution stage; the winner inputs build well-formed namespaced markup (e.g. xlink:href attribute / DTD-driven structure) rather than collapsing into the prefix==URI==NULL fast path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds exercise the namespace loop and namespace-resolution path: at L2321 the loop body runs (W: T=6) and at the blocker L2383 the true side fires (W T=6 F=9). Winner seed bytes contain structured namespace markup such as 'xlink:href' confirming a prefixed namespaced element with a URI.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds never take the blocker true side (L2383 L: T=0 F=161) and the prefix-match assignment at L2332 never fires for the loser (L: T=0), so URI/prefix never align to reach element-namespace resolution; the loser inputs do not converge on the well-formed prefixed-namespace shape.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient on top of cmplog's I2S. Since both fuzzers already have I2S, the differentiator is the CMP_MAP gradient: it gives partial-credit feedback on the byte-by-byte prefix/URI string comparisons (e.g. the prefix == pref pointer/identity and the surrounding namespace-name comparisons) so value_profile_cmplog climbs incrementally toward valid namespaced markup that cmplog's all-or-nothing edge signal cannot bridge.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog with the differentiating technique being value_profile.
The blocked true side requires URI != NULL and ret->ns == NULL at element-namespace resolution.
The winner takes the blocker true side six times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2383  T=6 F=9  T=0 F=161  if ((URI != NULL) && (ret->ns == NULL)) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner runs the namespace-build loop body where the prefix==pref comparison and ns assignment occur, but the loser does not match.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2332  T=6 F=0  T=0 F=3  if ((URI != NULL) && (prefix == pref))

evidence_trail.claim: A winner-resolving seed contains structured namespaced markup including an xlink:href attribute.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's CMP buffer and edge counters.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both involved fuzzers carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot be the differentiator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. Both cmplog and value_profile_cmplog already carry I2SRandReplace per MECHANISM CONTEXT, so a dash-row in lineage would not distinguish winner from loser; the differentiator is the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback that only value_profile_cmplog adds, which is a feedback-side signal not recorded as a mutation_op in seed metadata.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating a branch behind a multi-byte prefix/URI string comparison where value_profile_cmplog shows no advantage over cmplog (equal resolve rates across the scan), indicating the CMP_MAP gradient is not what bridges the comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section is highly dispersed (mostly PARTIAL offsets), so the exact prefix/URI string driving the L2332 prefix==pref match is inferred from the loop-structure divergence and one xlink:href seed rather than a concentrated dataflow offset.
